overall_summary: elizaOS has officially launched 'waifu', the first economic agent launchpad, marking a significant milestone in agent-powered infrastructure. Simultaneously, the framework transition to v2.0.2 is stabilizing with a major focus on the 'Owner+Agent' identity model and local inference optimizations for Intel hardware.
key_facts: Eliza launched 'waifu' (waifu.fun), an economic agent launchpad built by an agent named Sol the Architect and elizaOS v3 agents.
The migration period from ai16z to ElizaOS has officially ended, and the migration team has moved on to other processes.
ElizaOS v2.0.2 was released, introducing the 'Owner+Agent' identity architecture to manage distinct user and agent roles.
The official highest liquidity for ElizaOS tokens is currently located on the Binance Smart Chain (BSC).
Local inference performance issues were identified for Qwen2.5-14B models on Intel Lunar Lake iGPUs, showing only 13% bandwidth efficiency.
PR #7936 restored the 'milady-tails' Linux variant subtree, including 6,157 files for the Tails-based OS fork.
The Jupiter integration was migrated to the official API endpoint (api.jup.ag) with mandatory security key authentication.
A new 'vault' feature (@elizaos/vault) was introduced for secure, cross-platform secrets management.
open_questions: What is the ElizaOS roadmap for the coming months?
Will the 'milady-tails' variant eventually support RISC-V to meet the 'one version one build' requirement?
Can the speculative decoding harness be updated with M-RoPE awareness for Qwen3.5 architectures?

twitter_news_highlights.claim: The launch of waifu, the first economic agent launchpad, was detailed via Twitter posts by satsbased.

new_issues_prs.title: Restore packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails — per Shaw's go-ahead
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Restores the encrypted USB persistence and Tails-hardened security features for the OS variant.

new_issues_prs.title: vulkan: Qwen2.5-14B Q4_K_M bandwidth-efficiency cliff on Lunar Lake iGPU
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Identifies critical performance bottlenecks in local inference for middle-tier models on Intel hardware.

new_issues_prs.title: chore(release): develop → main (v2.0.2)
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Major version release stabilizing the agent runtime and cloud platform fixes.

overall_focus.claim: Consolidating the plugin ecosystem toward ESM-first architecture and hardening the agent runtime for headless server environments.

discord_updates.summary: Focus on the launch of 'waifu', the first economic agent launchpad, and technical details regarding its construction by Sol the Architect.
Clarification on token liquidity (BSC) and the definitive end of the ai16z-to-ElizaOS migration window.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users expressed interest in future product roadmaps and were seeking clarification on where to trade with maximum liquidity.

strategic_insights.insight: The move from singular agents to an agent launchpad (waifu) suggests a strategic shift toward a platform model where users can deploy financialized agents at scale.
implications_or_questions: Does the framework have sufficient security guardrails for high-frequency economic agents?

strategic_insights.insight: Intense benchmarking on Lunar Lake and RISC-V indicates an intent to deeply optimize elizaOS for edge computing and consumer-grade hardware rather than just cloud GPUs.
implications_or_questions: Will native NPU support become a requirement for the standard agent distribution?

market_analysis.observation: ElizaOS liquidity transitioned to BSC (Binance Smart Chain) as the primary hub following the migration.
market_analysis.relevance: Token holders need to be aware of the migration completion and liquidity concentration to avoid slippage or invalid claims on legacy chains.
